description: This command is used to apply boundary conditions at surface faces of the model. To apply conditions to zones rather than at faces, see the zone apply command. To apply conditions at individual gridpoints, see the zone gridpoint fix command.

6.0.reason: Not listed in the official FLAC3D 6.0 command indexes.

7.0.description: This command is used to apply boundary conditions at surface faces of the model. To apply conditions to zones rather than at faces, see the zone apply command. To apply conditions at individual gridpoints, see the zone gridpoint fix command.

keywords.name: discharge
keywords.description: Fluid flux f is the component of the specific discharge vector (e.g., in m/s) applied normal to the boundary.

keywords.name: leakage
keywords.description: f1 is the pore pressure in the leaky layer.

keywords.name: reaction
keywords.description: Vector of all components of reaction force at a gridpoint Reaction forces will only be applied in directions that are currently fixed, and those fixity conditions will be removed. This is a gridpoint based condition.

keywords.name: reaction-dip
keywords.description: Reaction force in the dip direction of the local gridpoint axes Reaction forces will only be applied in directions that are currently fixed, and those fixity conditions will be removed. This is a gridpoint based condition.

keywords.name: reaction-local
keywords.description: Apply a reaction force in all components of the local coordinate system (see Local Axes System below). Reaction forces will only be applied in directions that are currently fixed, and those fixity conditions will be removed. This is a gridpoint based condition.

keywords.name: reaction-normal
keywords.description: Reaction force in the normal direction of the local gridpoint axes (see Local Axes System below). Reaction forces will only be applied in directions that are currently fixed, and those fixity conditions will be removed. This is a gridpoint based condition.

keywords.name: reaction-strike
keywords.description: Reaction force in the strike direction of the local gridpoint axes (see Local Axes System below). Reaction forces will only be applied in directions that are currently fixed, and those fixity conditions will be removed. This is a gridpoint based condition.

keywords.name: reaction-x
keywords.description: \(x\)-component of reaction force at a gridpoint Reaction forces will only be applied in directions that are currently fixed, and those fixity conditions will be removed. This is a gridpoint based condition.

keywords.name: reaction-y
keywords.description: \(y\)-component of reaction force at a gridpoint Reaction forces will only be applied in directions that are currently fixed, and those fixity conditions will be removed. This is a gridpoint based condition.

keywords.name: reaction-z
keywords.description: \(z\)-component of reaction force at a gridpoint Reaction forces will only be applied in directions that are currently fixed, and those fixity conditions will be removed. This is a gridpoint based condition.

keywords.name: stress-dip
keywords.description: Stress component applied in the dip direction of the local face axes (see Local Axes System below).

keywords.name: stress-normal
keywords.description: Stress component applied in the normal direction of the local face axes (see Local Axes System below).

keywords.name: stress-strike
keywords.description: Stress component applied in the strike direction of the local face axes (see Local Axes System below).

keywords.name: stress-xx
keywords.description: xx-component of the stress tensor applied at a face

keywords.name: stress-xy
keywords.description: xy-component of the stress tensor applied at a face

keywords.name: stress-xz
keywords.description: xz-component of the stress tensor applied at a face

keywords.name: stress-yy
keywords.description: yy-component of the stress tensor applied at a face

keywords.name: stress-yz
keywords.description: xz-component of the stress tensor applied at a face

keywords.name: stress-zz
keywords.description: zz-component of the stress tensor applied at a face

keywords.name: velocity
keywords.description: Vector of all velocity components in the global coordinate axes. This is a gridpoint based condition.

keywords.name: velocity-dip
keywords.description: Velocity component applied in the dip direction of the local gridpoint axes (see Local Axes System below). This is a gridpoint based condition.

keywords.name: velocity-local
keywords.description: Vector of all velocity components in the local coordinate axes (see Local Axes System below). This is a gridpoint based condition.

keywords.name: velocity-normal
keywords.description: Velocity component applied in the normal direction of the local gridpoint axes (see Local Axes System below). This is a gridpoint based condition.

keywords.name: velocity-strike
keywords.description: Velocity component applied in the strike direction of the local gridpoint axes (see Local Axes System below). This is a gridpoint based condition.

keywords.name: velocity-x
keywords.description: \(x\)-component of velocity applied at a gridpoint This is a gridpoint based condition.

keywords.name: velocity-y
keywords.description: \(y\)-component of velocity applied at a gridpoint This is a gridpoint based condition.

keywords.name: velocity-z
keywords.description: \(z\)-component of velocity applied at a gridpoint This is a gridpoint based condition.

keywords.name: fish
keywords.description: Specify a multiplier that is a FISH function named s. The returned value of this FISH function is multiplied by the base value (including gradient) over all zone faces or gridpoints included in this apply condition. So a return value of 1.0 will apply the base value provided, and a return value of 0.0 will effectively remove it. This function will be called every step. If the apply condition requires a vector value, this function should also return a vector. If the apply condition requires two values, the function should return a vector and the \(z\)-component will be ignored.

keywords.name: fish-local
keywords.description: Specify a multiplier that is a FISH function named s, which is applied separately to each individual zone face or gridpoint affected by this apply condition. This function must take two arguments. For apply conditions that work directly on faces, the first argument is a pointer to the zone and the second argument is an integer from 1 to 6 indicating the face. For apply conditions that work on gridpoints, the first argument is a pointer to the gridpoint and the second argument is not used. This allows an apply condition to vary over time as well as space. The returned value of this FISH function is multiplied by the base value (including gradient) on the specific zone supplied. So a return value of 1.0 will apply the base value provided, and a return value of 0.0 will effectively remove it. This function will be called every step for every zone included in the apply condition. If the apply condition requires a vector value, this function should also return a vector. If the apply condition requires two values, the function should return a vector and the \(z\)-component will be ignored.

keywords.name: gradient
keywords.description: Apply a gradient to the scalar-value provided. This is not available if the apply conditions requires two values or a vector value.

keywords.name: servo
keywords.description: Use a servo tied to the mechanical convergence ratio to control the magnitude of the applied condition. This can be used to maintain, gradually ramp up, or gradually reduce apply conditions while maintaining a quasi-static response. This controls a factor that is multiplied with the base value supplied. By default the factor will start at 0.001, and will gradually increase to 1.0 as the convergence ratio falls below 2e-3, and decrease if it rises above 1e-2. The following keywords are available to control the servo response:

keywords.name: servo-latency
keywords.description: The minimum number of steps that must pass since the last servo adjustment before the next one occurs. This can keep the servo from over controlling in the initial response to a change in the factor. The default value is 1.

keywords.name: servo-lower-bound
keywords.description: Set the lower mechanical convergence ratio limit. If the current ratio falls below this limit, the factor will be multiplied by the lower-multiplier. The default limit is 2e-3.

keywords.name: servo-lower-multiplier
keywords.description: If the current ratio falls below the lower-bound limit, the factor is multiplied by this number. The default is 1.01.

keywords.name: servo-maximum
keywords.description: The maximum value the factor is allowed to become. The default is 1.0.

keywords.name: servo-minimum
keywords.description: The minimum value the factor is allowed to become. The default is 0.001.

keywords.name: servo-ramp
keywords.description: Sets the servo into ramp mode. This means that the factor is not allowed to go down, the value will only increase. This is useful to gradually increase an applied condition to it’s full value. See also the reduce keyword.

keywords.name: servo-ratio
keywords.description: Which mechanical convergence ratio is compared against lower-bound and upper-bound. By default this is the current value assigned by the zone ratio command (which is average by default. See the zone ratio command for definitions. The available keywords are:

keywords.name: servo-ratio-average
keywords.description: Use the average mechanical force ratio.

keywords.name: servo-ratio-maximum
keywords.description: Use the maximum mechanical force ratio.

keywords.name: servo-ratio-local
keywords.description: Use the local mechanical force ratio.

keywords.name: servo-reduce
keywords.description: Sets the servo into reduce mode. In this mode the starting value of the factor is 1.0, and the minimum value is set to 0.0. When the current ratio falls below lower-bound the factor is reduced by the upper-multiplier. The factor is never increased. This is useful to gradually decrease an applied condition to zero.

keywords.name: servo-upper-bound
keywords.description: Set the upper mechanical convergence ratio value. If the current ratio rises above this value, the factor will be multiplier by the upper-multiplier. The default is 1e-2.

keywords.name: servo-upper-multiplier
keywords.description: If the current ratio rises above the upper-bound, the factor is multiplied by this. The default is 0.975.

keywords.name: system
keywords.description: The local coordinate system used by the apply condition may be specified explicitly by the user. By default, this coordinate system is determined automatically. For faces it is determined by the normal direction of the face. For gridpoints it is determined by the average normal vectors of all faces connected to the gridpoint that are a part of this apply condition.

keywords.name: system-normal
keywords.description: v is the unit normal vector to the plane.

keywords.name: system-dip
keywords.description: dip [degrees], f, of the plane measured in the negative z-direction from the global xy-plane.

keywords.name: system-dip-direction
keywords.description: dip-direction [degrees], f, of the plane measured in the global xy-plane clockwise from the positive y-axis.

keywords.name: table
keywords.description: Specify a multiplier that is a table named s. By default the x-axis value is the current step number. The optional time keyword may be used to specify which processes accumulated time-total should be used to provide the x-axis value. The available keywords are:

keywords.name: vary
keywords.description: Apply a linear variation to the scalar-value provided. This is not available if the apply conditions requires two values or a vector value.

keywords.name: acceleration
keywords.description: Apply acceleration boundary condition (vector form).

keywords.name: acceleration-dip
keywords.description: Apply acceleration in the dip direction. (3D only.)

keywords.name: acceleration-local
keywords.description: Apply acceleration in the gridpoint-local coordinate system.

keywords.name: acceleration-normal
keywords.description: Apply acceleration normal to the face.

keywords.name: acceleration-strike
keywords.description: Apply acceleration in the strike direction. (3D only.)

keywords.name: acceleration-x
keywords.description: Apply acceleration x-component (global frame).

keywords.name: acceleration-y
keywords.description: Apply acceleration y-component (global frame).

keywords.name: acceleration-z
keywords.description: Apply acceleration z-component (global frame). (3D only.)

keywords.name: convection
keywords.description: Apply convective heat-transfer condition. f1 = ambient temperature, f2 = heat-transfer coefficient. (Thermal config.)

keywords.name: discharge
keywords.description: Apply prescribed fluid discharge. (Fluid config.)

keywords.name: flux
keywords.description: Apply prescribed heat flux. (Thermal config.)

keywords.name: head
keywords.description: Apply prescribed pore-pressure head. (Fluid config.)

keywords.name: leakage
keywords.description: Apply leakage boundary condition. (Fluid config.)

keywords.name: pore-pressure
keywords.description: Apply prescribed pore pressure. (Fluid config.)

keywords.name: quiet
keywords.description: Apply a quiet (non-reflecting) boundary across all components. (Dynamic config.)

keywords.name: quiet-dip
keywords.description: Apply a quiet boundary in the dip component only. (3D, dynamic config.)

keywords.name: quiet-normal
keywords.description: Apply a quiet boundary in the normal component only. (Dynamic config.)

keywords.name: quiet-strike
keywords.description: Apply a quiet boundary in the strike component only. (3D, dynamic config.)

keywords.name: reaction
keywords.description: Reaction-force boundary across all components.

keywords.name: reaction-dip
keywords.description: Reaction force in the dip component. (3D only.)

keywords.name: reaction-local
keywords.description: Reaction force in the gridpoint-local frame.

keywords.name: reaction-normal
keywords.description: Reaction force normal to the face.

keywords.name: reaction-strike
keywords.description: Reaction force in the strike direction. (3D only.)

keywords.name: reaction-x
keywords.description: Reaction force x-component (global frame).

keywords.name: reaction-y
keywords.description: Reaction force y-component (global frame).

keywords.name: reaction-z
keywords.description: Reaction force z-component (global frame). (3D only.)

keywords.name: stress-dip
keywords.description: Apply traction in the dip direction. (3D only.)

keywords.name: stress-normal
keywords.description: Apply traction normal to the face.

keywords.name: stress-strike
keywords.description: Apply traction in the strike direction. (3D only.)

keywords.name: stress-tensor
keywords.description: Apply the full stress tensor (six components) as a face traction condition.

keywords.name: stress-xx
keywords.description: Traction tensor xx-component.

keywords.name: stress-xy
keywords.description: Traction tensor xy-component.

keywords.name: stress-xz
keywords.description: Traction tensor xz-component. (3D only.)

keywords.name: stress-yy
keywords.description: Traction tensor yy-component.

keywords.name: stress-yz
keywords.description: Traction tensor yz-component. (3D only.)

keywords.name: stress-zz
keywords.description: Traction tensor zz-component. (3D only.)

keywords.name: temperature
keywords.description: Apply prescribed temperature. (Thermal config.)

keywords.name: velocity
keywords.description: Apply velocity boundary condition (vector form).

keywords.name: velocity-dip
keywords.description: Velocity in the dip direction. (3D only.)

keywords.name: velocity-local
keywords.description: Velocity in the gridpoint-local frame.

keywords.name: velocity-normal
keywords.description: Velocity normal to the face.

keywords.name: velocity-strike
keywords.description: Velocity in the strike direction. (3D only.)

keywords.name: velocity-x
keywords.description: Velocity x-component (global frame).

keywords.name: velocity-y
keywords.description: Velocity y-component (global frame).

keywords.name: velocity-z
keywords.description: Velocity z-component (global frame). (3D only.)

keywords.name: acceleration-tangential
keywords.description: Apply acceleration tangential to the face. (2D only.)

keywords.name: quiet-tangential
keywords.description: Quiet boundary, tangential component. (2D, dynamic config.)

keywords.name: reaction-tangential
keywords.description: Reaction force, tangential component. (2D only.)

keywords.name: stress-shear
keywords.description: Apply shear traction. (2D only.)

keywords.name: velocity-tangential
keywords.description: Velocity in the tangential direction. (2D only.)

keywords.name: fish
keywords.description: Use the FISH function named s to compute the applied value per face/gridpoint.

keywords.name: fish-local
keywords.description: Use the local-namespace FISH function s to compute the applied value.

keywords.name: gradient
keywords.description: Apply a linear spatial gradient v to the base value (optionally with a reference origin).

keywords.name: vary
keywords.description: Apply a linear variation v to the base value across the face.

keywords.name: update-direction
keywords.description: If on, the direction of the applied vector quantity is updated each step to follow the current face normal (for large-strain analyses).
